name: Broiled Orange Cake
recipeInstructions:
FOR THE CAKE:
Cream the butter, add sugar, and beat well. Add the beaten egg and beat until smooth.
Sift together the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, and salt) and gradually add to the wet mixture with buttermilk. Alternate adding the dry mix with the liquid, ensuring a smooth batter.
Fold in the raisins, orange rind, and nuts if desired. Pour the batter into an 8 x 11 x 2-inch pan. Place the oven in the cold position and set it to 325°F. Bake until well done, about 1 hour. Check for doneness with a toothpick; the cake is ready when the toothpick comes out clean.
FOR THE TOPPING:
Combine the orange and lemon juices with sugar and melted butter. Pour this mixture over the warm cake. Place under the broiler until the topping just begins to brown slightly, but be careful not to burn it. Serve the cake warm or cold with whipped cream. Keep the cake in its pan to retain moisture.
Source: "Mountain Measures" - Junior League of Charleston, WV edition, 1974
